definition: Total price return over the most recent 12-month period, including dividends if adjusted close prices are used
formula: (P_end / P_start) - 1 where the window is exactly 252 trading days
required_input_fields: adjusted close price; 1 year of daily data
interpretation: Higher is better; compare vs STOXX 600 benchmark
limitations: Point-in-time; sensitive to start/end date; does not adjust for risk

definition: Annualised total price return over 3 years
required_input_fields: adjusted close price; 3 years of daily data
interpretation: Captures a medium-term cycle; less sensitive to single-year anomaly
limitations: Requires 3 years of price history; may include COVID distortion

definition: Annualised total price return over 5 years
required_input_fields: adjusted close price; 5 years of daily data
interpretation: Best measure of long-run compounding ability
limitations: Requires 5 years of price history; older data may reflect different business model

definition: Standard deviation of daily log-returns annualised by sqrt(252)
required_input_fields: adjusted close price; minimum 1 year of daily data
interpretation: Lower is better for risk-adjusted returns; >40% triggers volatility exclusion in this pipeline
limitations: Does not capture tail risk or skewness; historical vol may not predict future vol

definition: Maximum peak-to-trough percentage decline over the measurement window
formula: (trough / peak) - 1; peak = rolling max; trough = rolling min after peak
required_input_fields: adjusted close price; minimum 1 year of daily data
interpretation: Measures worst-case loss scenario; more negative = higher risk
limitations: Depends heavily on measurement window; drawdown may not be fully recovered

definition: Excess return per unit of total risk; risk-free rate assumed 0% in this pipeline
required_input_fields: annual_return_pct; annualized_volatility_pct
interpretation: Higher is better; >0.5 considered acceptable; <0 triggers exclusion in this pipeline
limitations: Assumes normal return distribution; zero risk-free rate assumption is a simplification

definition: Sensitivity of stock returns to benchmark (STOXX Europe 600) movements; proxy EXW1.DE
required_input_fields: adjusted close price for stock; adjusted close price for EXW1.DE (benchmark); minimum 1 year overlap
interpretation: Beta >1.5 triggers exclusion in this pipeline; beta ~1 = market-neutral; beta <1 = defensive
limitations: Benchmark proxy EXW1.DE may not perfectly replicate STOXX 600; beta is backwards-looking

definition: Most recently reported annual revenue (total net sales or equivalent)
formula: Direct from financial statements — no formula; use reported figure
required_input_fields: Annual report income statement; yfinance .info trailing_revenue
unit: EUR millions (convert if reported in other currency)
interpretation: Baseline for margin calculations and growth analysis
limitations: Currency conversion required for non-EUR reporters; restatements may affect comparability

definition: Compound annual growth rate of revenue over 3 years
required_input_fields: Revenue for current year and 3 years prior
interpretation: Positive = growing top line; >5% considered healthy in most sectors
limitations: Acquisitions can inflate growth; organic growth preferred but harder to source

definition: EBIT as a percentage of revenue; measures operating profitability before interest and tax
required_input_fields: EBIT and Revenue from income statement
preferred_source: yfinance .info (ebitda - D&A approximation) or annual report
interpretation: Higher is better; sector benchmarking required; <5% may indicate margin pressure
limitations: Not applicable to banks or insurers — use sector-specific equivalents; EBIT may not be separately reported

definition: EBITDA as a percentage of revenue; adds back depreciation and amortisation
required_input_fields: EBITDA (reported or EBIT + D&A) and Revenue
interpretation: Proxy for operating cash generation; commonly used for EV multiples
limitations: Excludes capex — can flatter asset-heavy businesses; not a GAAP measure

definition: Net income as a percentage of revenue; measures bottom-line profitability after all costs
required_input_fields: Net Income and Revenue from income statement
interpretation: Higher is better; negative = loss-making; cyclical businesses will vary
limitations: Affected by non-recurring items; tax jurisdictions vary across portfolio

definition: Total financial debt minus cash and equivalents; a negative value means net cash position
formula: Total Debt - Cash and Cash Equivalents
required_input_fields: totalDebt; totalCash from yfinance .info or balance sheet
interpretation: Net cash is a positive sign; high net debt relative to EBITDA indicates leverage risk
limitations: Does not capture off-balance-sheet liabilities (leases under IFRS 16 may or may not be included)

definition: Leverage ratio showing how many years of EBITDA would be needed to repay net debt
required_input_fields: net_debt; EBITDA
interpretation: <2x considered low leverage; >4x considered high; negative = net cash
limitations: EBITDA denominator can be distorted by non-cash items; negative EBITDA makes ratio meaningless

definition: EBIT relative to interest expense; measures ability to service debt
required_input_fields: EBIT; interest expense from income statement
interpretation: >3x generally safe; <1.5x signals financial stress; not applicable to banks/insurers,Interest expense can be difficult to separate from other financial costs in condensed statements"

definition: Free cash flow as a percentage of revenue; measures cash conversion of profits
required_input_fields: operatingCashflow; capitalExpenditures; totalRevenue from yfinance .info
interpretation: Positive FCF margin = self-funding; negative = cash-burning; high FCF margin = capital-light business
limitations: Capex classification varies; maintenance vs growth capex not always separated

definition: Price-to-earnings ratio; stock price divided by earnings per share
formula: Market Cap / Net Income (or Share Price / EPS)
required_input_fields: Market cap or share price; net income or EPS
interpretation: Lower = cheaper relative to earnings; >30x considered expensive in most sectors; negative if loss-making
limitations: Highly sensitive to earnings cyclicality; negative earnings make ratio meaningless; use EV/EBITDA as backup

definition: Enterprise value to EBITDA; most common cross-sector valuation multiple
required_input_fields: Market cap; net_debt; EBITDA
interpretation: <10x often considered fair value; >20x considered expensive; sector-specific benchmarks required
limitations: Not meaningful for negative EBITDA; sector norms vary significantly

definition: Enterprise value to revenue; useful when margins are low or negative
required_input_fields: Market cap; net_debt; Revenue
interpretation: Lower = cheaper relative to revenue; useful for growth companies without positive earnings
limitations: Less informative for mature profitable businesses; heavily influenced by sector capital intensity

definition: Annual dividends per share as a percentage of current share price
required_input_fields: dividendRate or dividendYield from yfinance .info
interpretation: Higher yield can indicate income generation or value; very high yield may signal distress
limitations: Zero yield for growth companies is not negative; yield changes with share price

definition: Composite score (0–100) combining return profile, risk profile, profitability, balance sheet, and valuation. Higher = better.
formula: Weighted average of sub-scores: Return 25%, Risk 25%, Profitability 25%, Balance sheet 15%, Valuation 10%. All sub-scores normalised to 0-100 within universe.
required_input_fields: All above metrics required; missing metrics reduce confidence and trigger flag
interpretation: Scores are relative within this portfolio universe, not absolute
limitations: Score is a tool for ranking within this universe only; not a recommendation; missing data degrades accuracy

definition: Qualitative summary of financial risk level based on leverage, volatility, coverage, and drawdown
formula: LOW if: volatility <25%, net debt/EBITDA <2x, Sharpe >0.5, coverage >3x. HIGH if any exclusion threshold breached. MEDIUM otherwise.
required_input_fields: annualized_volatility; net_debt_to_ebitda; sharpe_ratio; interest_coverage
interpretation: HIGH flag does not automatically exclude — triggers human review
limitations: Rules are simplified; human judgement required for final classification

definition: Qualitative valuation assessment relative to sector medians
formula: CHEAP if EV/EBITDA and P/E both below sector median. EXPENSIVE if both above 1.5x sector median. FAIR otherwise. UNKNOWN if data missing.
required_input_fields: pe_ratio; ev_ebitda; sector comparables
interpretation: Flags for human review only — valuation is not an exclusion criterion in this pipeline
limitations: Sector comparables required; not yet implemented automatically — human review needed

definition: Overall confidence in the financial data for this company. Reflects data completeness, recency, and consistency.
formula: HIGH: all key metrics present and sourced from primary data. MEDIUM: some metrics missing or from secondary source. LOW: significant gaps or sector metric adjustment required.
required_input_fields: All above metrics
interpretation: LOW confidence does not prevent inclusion — triggers human verification requirement
limitations: Confidence reflects data availability, not financial quality
